title: alsine douglasii
intro: Alsine douglasii, also known as Douglas' crowberry, is a low-growing, woody shrub native to the mountainous regions of western North America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Alsine douglasii are believed to include antioxidant and antitumor activities, as well as potential applications in the treatment of digestive issues and wounds. Alsine douglasii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
